programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Amanda tries to help a famous Chinese ballet dancer defect to America, but her efforts threaten to cause an international incident - and Dr Sloan finds himself caught in the diplomatic crossfire. Medical mystery, starring Dick Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble discovers that negligence caused the death of a salvage diver and jeopardises his own life when he befriends the girl accused of being a jinx

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The lawman battles a formidable arms dealer in a fight to the death

programme.title: CSI: NY

Meanwhile, a link emerges between the cases of a scantily clad woman hit by a lorry and a baseball fan who died after the defeat of his favourite team. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A deep-sea diver is murdered on the job, and his co-workers all become suspects. However, when they return to the surface, they must remain in a high-tech decompression chamber for four days. With each of their suspects confined within a tank, the NCIS team must complete an unorthodox investigation from the outside. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when a diplomatic service van is hijacked during the transportation of an unknown package from the State Department. As Callen and Sam celebrate their fifth anniversary as partners, the pair undertake their trickiest undercover operation to date. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine's teeth and fingertips are found in a pickpocket's stolen purse, and Ducky discovers he had been tortured on a rack before dying. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when two pharmaceutical executives are murdered, uncovering a criminal who changes his identity for each offence he commits, and has evaded capture across several countries. To make matters worse, the agents are made to feel the pressure by the watchful eye of Granger. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and Miguel Ferrer

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Vance sticks rigidly to the rules as he investigates the death of a fellow agent, but Gibbs and Franks take matters into their own hands to catch the killers. programme.desc: After the new mayor, Una Drake, launches an initiative to clean up the town of Lighthaven, she forcibly closes the Punch and Judy stall. Later, the man behind the stall is found dead on the beach, and when Jane meets with Una, she finds her rude and abrupt. However, in the small hours, Davey and Jane are called to the scene of a second death - the mayor has been found beaten in a hotel room. The pair search for a link between the two deaths, and Jane's quest for the truth puts her life in danger. Crime drama, starring Claire Goose

Guest starring Paul Rhys

programme.title: Ffit Cymru

programme.title: Gwyl Lleisiau Eraill

This year, the festival had to move from its home in Cardigan to the National Museum in Cardiff. There was no audience, but instead an eclectic feast of music from Ani Glass, the Gentle Good, Catrin Finch and Aoife Ni Bhriain, Juice Menace and Berwyn

programme.desc: In Christchurch, Dorset, Charlie and the Rich brothers have a £3,000 budget to turn a young family's ugly concrete space into a beautiful, colourful garden that promotes recycling

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Basil's belief that guests are an obstacle to the smooth running of his hotel is borne out by the arrival of an aggressive woman (Joan Sanderson), and the tension between the two leads to a series of increasingly heated arguments. Classic comedy, starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales, Andrew Sachs and Connie Booth

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney tells Callum she believes Ben was involved in Kush's death, and despite assuring her that his fiance is innocent, Callum begins to waver when he overhears Ben planning a secret meeting. Mitch and Karen feel as if they are under suspicion as the police ask to search their house for information on Bailey and Linda cannot accept Nancy's decision, leaving Mick to defuse the situation

programme.title: Sully - Miracle on the Hudson (2016)
programme.desc: Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, starring Tom Hanks as pilot Chesley 'Sully' Sullenberger, who rose to fame in January 2009 when he safely landed US Airways Flight 1549 in New York's Hudson River after the plane lost all engine power shortly after take-off - an event that captured the imagination of the world. Also starring Aaron Eckhart, Laura Linney and Anna Gunn

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: Three months after Jessie fled Tom's flat, a chance encounter clears the air. As they enjoy their long awaited second date, Tom cannot help but listen to outside warnings about their possible romance. Minnie Driver guest stars

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: After a messy break-up Ella and Tizzy have never discussed whether he cheated whilst on tour - until now. After two years Margo is desperate to find out why they were dumped by ex Felix. Childhood sweethearts Molly and Archie want to salvage their friendship but first they must reveal whether either of them cheated

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: Australian and New Zealand drag queens compete to be the first Drag Race superstar from Down Under. Michelle Visage and Rhys Nicholson are on hand to judge the performances

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The second of two compilations of highlights from the recent series, featuring appearances by Tom Cruise, Rebel Wilson, Rege-Jean Page, Rosamund Pike, Tom Jones, Keeley Hawes, Ian Wright, Sienna Miller, Sam Neill, Kate Winslet, Alan Carr, Felicity Kendal, Adrian Lester, Amy Poehler, Daniel Kaluuya, Jennifer Garner, Stanley Tucci, Cush Jumbo, Liam Neeson, Octavia Spencer, Melissa McCarthy and Orlando Bloom

programme.title: Rip Off Britain

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin looks round a chalet-style bungalow in the Derbyshire village of Kilburn and a three-bedroom end terrace in Huthwaite, Nottinghamshire that offers a colourful reminder of his footballing past. Plus, a revisit to a church in Dover that Martin Roberts first saw five years ago

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Two police officers and an air stewardess perform CPR on a lorry driver who has had a cardiac arrest at the wheel. A mother calls mountain rescue when her son falls from a tree

programme.desc: Anita Manning visits the Elsecar Heritage Centre in South Yorkshire, where Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley help two teams scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction. Anita also learns about a local man who changed the face of mining in Britain

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi helps a family struggling with the long-term physical and emotional effects of cancer over the course of a decade. Adrian Lewis Morgan stars

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple of Londoners who have £1million to spend on a detached property in the Essex countryside with lots of space for family and friends. Away from the search, Ginny visits a farmer who has found that the county's unique climate has enabled him to grow a crop usually found much further afield

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: JJ Chalmers is joined by artist Chloe Kempster and blacksmith Kev Paxton as they try to transform an unwanted writing bureau, car mechanic ramps and a hardwood coffee table

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a pair of binoculars salvaged from a Second World War U-Boat, two leather boxing belts and a vintage mechanical money box

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Labour candidate for the London mayoral election.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A furious Ben demands answers from Callum after his confession. Linda speaks to Nancy's doctor, but her meddling only makes things worse between mother and daughter. Keegan makes an error that could jeopardise the search for Bailey and Kim assesses Bobby's Fox Catcher application. Despite only being here five minutes, Stuart's grandma Vi seems to be getting on everyone's nerves - although she insists that Walford is growing on her

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The cause of Reyhan's death prompts an investigation, putting Hanssen and Sahira's already brittle relationship at breaking point. The success of Dominic's operation gives him a new-found sense of hope for the future, while Kian continues to lie to Lucky about notifying social services regarding Andrei

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate the case of a man who thought he had found love on a dating site with a woman who began making demands for money

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The gang arrive back in England, devastated that their trip to Monaco has been all for nothing. However, as they leave the airport, they find an unexpected welcome party waiting for them with big news. Keeley realises that Jake may still has feelings for Georgina, and as Frank's lies begin to unravel, he begs Cheryl to help him in a last-ditch attempt to save himself and Duke

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The hopefuls are given a challenge by Cate Hall, the lead hair and make-up designer on award-winning drama The Crown, demonstrating their knowledge of period make-up to create authentic and recognisable looks for a screen test. In the creative brief, they are challenged to turn their models into a living piece of art

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: A celebration of the entrepreneurial spirit among the young. This opening episode features Nanial Jones, who invested all his savings into a vintage clothing business. Plus, Alaw Haf who now makes £5,000 a month from subscriptions on the OnlyFans website, having lost her gym job in lockdown, and Toni Leigh Hall who has launched a new line of humorous cards, calendars, door-mats, mouse-mats and face masks

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: The prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho returns, with Emma and Norman each making a decision about the future, and Norma setting boundaries for her son. An unexpected visitor puts Dylan's fresh start in jeopardy. Starring Freddie Highmore and Vera Farmiga

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: The mother and son fixate on Annika's disappearance, and Norma uncovers the truth about the Arcanum Club. Dylan and Caleb meet their new neighbour. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga, Freddie Highmore and Max Thieriot

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford hears how some missing parcels can end up being sold off at auction instead of safely delivered. Plus, the rural communities who struggled to get quality broadband

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts explores an ambitious housing development in Tredegar, Dion Dublin visits a house in Great Barr and Martel Maxwell is impressed by a six-bedroom terrace in Liverpool

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works with one of the UK's biggest water companies. He traces the flow of this precious resource from the reservoir to homes and businesses, before finding out what happens with wastewater at the other end of the system

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A father braves a house fire to save his daughter. A pregnant woman on the way to hospital does not make it, so her partner dials 999 when the baby begins to arrive in the car

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents as two teams search for antiques in Leominster, Herefordshire, led by experts Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks. Christina learns about 'The Leominster Chelsea Bun', which travelled to the battle fields of the First World War and back

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie faces a choice - does she want to a medical career or a life of showbusiness glitz and glamour? A young carer struggles to cope when his grandma's obsession with an old music hall performance gets out of hand

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson searches for a Cambridgeshire home for a young couple with a £570,000 budget who want to escape suburban Kent. Keen to explore more of the country's wildlife, Jules also visits the Fens and finds out about the UK's first nature reserves

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore enlists screen print designer Daniel Heath and clock restorer Paul Parry to rejuvenate an old writing bureau and a 1940s mantle clock. She also tries to cultivate something sellable from fragrant bags of garden waste

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Vintage bike restorer Tim Gunn brings all his skill to bear on a wartime bicycle once owned and used by a Bletchley Park code breaker. Horologist Steve Fletcher recruits fellow expert and sister Suzie to work on a neglected and battered old Victorian firefighter's helmet. A threadbare teddy on wheels is also restored

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Reporter Greg McKenzie meets young people born or brought up in the UK who say Home Office immigration policy treats them as second class citizens. They cannot vote, cannot claim benefits, struggle to go to university and may even face deportation. Panorama investigates Home Office policy which gives them a chance to stay in the UK, but which takes 10 years and costs thousands of pounds

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher works on a mantle clock in the shape of a ship, a treasured gift from the owner's late grandfather. Kirsten Ramsay is called upon to painstakingly piece together a vintage ceramic night light, fractured into dozens of fragments, and skateboard enthusiast Mike is hoping Will Kirk and Dominic Chinea can restore his wooden board, which transports him back to his youth in 1960s Liverpool. Finally, toy restorers Julie and Amanda undertake the repair of a treasured teddy bear that has been by the side of a young man as he's undergone life-changing surgery

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contestants go global, taking on garments and techniques from around the world. For the pattern challenge they make a French breton top - and judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young will be looking for perfectly matched stripes and a completely flat neck - before they transform sarongs into completely different garments. In the final round, the sewers must come up with an outfit inspired by Mexican artist Frida Kahlo, known as much for her personal style as for her vivid artwork. Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Unsolved: An Alibi for Omar?
programme.desc: Journalist Bronagh Munro uncovers new evidence casting doubt on the conviction of a man who has spent 19 years in prison for murder. Omar Benguit was jailed for killing 26-year-old Korean student Jong-Ok Shin in Bournemouth in 2002. Now, Bronagh tracks down some of the alleged witnesses to ask them what they remember. She also finds evidence that could give Omar Benguit an alibi for the night of the murder

programme.title: Boxing: Canelo Alvarez
programme.desc: A profile of the Mexican boxer, who has won world titles in four different weight classes

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Fleur East, as they assist two friends from London in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing acrobat, a princess and a karate champion

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood lifts the lid on what really happens to Britain's waste, finding out how non-recyclable rubbish is turned into energy to power homes and businesses

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph is at the Walsall Recycling Centre, where she digs out a bamboo chair uncovered from some garden waste, an unwanted pine corner unit, and a mid-century bedside table

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: It is training time for barn owls Rowan and Cedar, recently arrived from Colchester Zoo, while senior keeper Erika patiently attempts to win the confidence of Sumatran tigress Dharma. At Highland Wildlife Park, a member of the public enjoys the company of Arktos and Walker the male polar bears when she takes part in a Keeper for the Day experience

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the third session of the final, as the players face a potential eight frames in the tournament at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield. Ronnie O'Sullivan dominated last year's corresponding session against Kyren Wilson, losing the first frame before winning seven in a row to take a 17-8 lead in the match. That left the Rocket requiring just one more frame to clinch a sixth world crown, and end a relatively barren run in the tournament, including defeat in the first round in 2019. Wilson was playing in his first world final, and has established himself as a regular in the top eight of the world rankings this season

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed, Dom Joly, Angela Rippon and John Robins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores East Anglia between the wars, beginning at Sutton Hoo in Suffolk, where archaeologists discovered a 27-metre-long Anglo-Saxon ship in 1939. In the Essex village of Dedham, he unearths a nasty brush between painters, with art experts explaining the antipathy between traditional artist Alfred Munnings and the modern art school established in the village by Cedric Morris

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the fourth and concluding session of the final at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield, where the first player to reach 18 frames is crowned champion. This session lasted just 11 minutes in 2020, with Ronnie O'Sullivan quickly winning the one frame he needed to secure an 18-8 victory. The triumph was The Rocket's sixth world title, putting him one behind Stephen Hendry and level with Steve Davis and Ray Reardon on the all-time modern-day list

programme.title: Tulip Fever (2017)
programme.desc: In 17th-century Amsterdam at the height of tulip mania, an artist falls in love with a married woman whose portrait he has been commissioned to paint. Historical romance based on Deborah Moggach's novel, starring Alicia Vikander, Dane DeHaan and Jack O'Connell

programme.title: Bent Coppers: Crossing the Line of Duty
programme.desc: The final instalment reveals how, in the late 1970s, corruption within the City of London's CID had grown out of control - and how Operation Countryman was launched in response

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is in the Nidderdale Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty in North Yorkshire, helping prepare for the return of both animals and visitors to the hills. She joins a team keeping track of adders emerging from hibernation to find out why numbers are falling. On his Cotswold farm, Adam Henson's struggling with the unpredictable spring weather, and Tom Heap's investigates how a rise in pet ownership is fuelling dog thefts. Plus, wildlife film-maker Jack Perks celebrates the grayling

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Elizabeth Taylor's image is known around the world - she was one of the most famous women in Hollywood, but could just a handful of photos uncover new truths about someone we think we all know? This programme takes a journey through the actress's life in 10 photos, with their secrets revealed by those who were there and those who knew Elizabeth Taylor's story best

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit some of Britain's most sumptuous and unusual locations, attracting large crowds intent on finding out what their hidden treasures are worth

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins workers at the Port of Tyne, helping to unload cargo containers from the quayside, and discovering how their contents are transported around the country

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and her team tackle items including some retro kitchen cupboards, a set of old garden gates and a sideboard with matching chairs

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don takes viewers through the next steps of chilli seed growth. Joe Swift gets design inspiration from a courtyard garden, and Nick Bailey finds out about an annoying pest spoiling box hedges

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including Wigan Warriors v Hull FC, Leigh Centurions v St Helens and Salford Red Devils v Castleford Tigers

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: In Epsom, Surrey, Amy and Chris want their bathroom redone, while Sarie and Matthew in Sale, Greater Manchester, want to give their daughter's bedroom a makeover

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Arlo still has not told Lindsey about his feelings for Poppy, and the stress of knowing what he is up to is beginning to take its toll on Billy

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores the Namib in south-west Africa, the oldest desert in the world. With little to no rainfall, the animals that live here have to be tough and Steve examines the tactics that each employs to cope with the harsh conditions, before revealing the secret that allows life to survive at all in such an environment

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: An exploration of Australia's underwater ecosystem, one of the natural wonders of the world and the largest living structure on the planet. In the first of three programmes, marine biologist Monty Halls examines the complex form of the coral reef itself and the wildlife that lives on it, from deadly fish-hunting snails and sharks that can walk on land to fighting corals and parrotfish, which cocoon themselves every night

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A look back at some of the best finds made on the series, when experts including Michael Baggott, Anita Manning, Thomas Plant, Mark Stacey, David Harper and Catherine Southon located a gilded cherub and a collection of Victorian prints among many other items of interest. Plus, while in the grounds of Beaulieu Abbey in Hampshire, presenter Paul Martin recalls the story of the dissolution of the monasteries

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed, Dom Joly, Angela Rippon and John Robins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through East Anglia, where he discovers the Essex origins of the BBC and joins the Women's Land Army to pick damsons at Tiptree

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan hosts a contest in which eight talented jewellers battle it out to prove they can be the next big jewellery-making sensation. This time, the five remaining contestants are tasked to make a pair of identical gold hoop earrings, before making a gold locket for a real-world client. Who will impress the judges enough to stay to make it through to the semi-final?

programme.title: Makeup: A Glamorous History
programme.desc: In the final episode, makeup artist Lisa Eldridge explores her favourite era for makeup, beauty and glamour, Britain in the 1920s. Lisa visits the British Film Institute archives to find out what impact the arrival of Hollywood cinema had on the development of some of our favourite makeup products, before working with pharmacist Szu Shen Wong to recreate authentic period recipes for foundation, eye makeup and lipstick

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong guest hosts, inviting comedian Joe Lycett and journalist Kirsty Wark to join regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: The stories of ordinary people caught up in the biggest events in living memory, and the person who changed their lives. It begins with Karl, a trainee clinical psychologist who was a passenger on the first carriage of the Piccadilly Line London Underground train - one of the four targets of the 7/7 bombings. After the bomb exploded, Karl was comforted by a woman who held his hand in the smoke-filled carriage as he feared for his life. Cameras also follow Emina, an NHS researcher living in Nottingham. She and her younger sister Edina, who was diagnosed with Down Syndrome as a baby, were born in Sarajevo. Edina is searching for the doctor who helped her family evacuate to safety, and gave Edina the chance of a better life. Anita Rani presents

programme.title: Bent Coppers: Crossing the Line of Duty
programme.desc: Exploring the sleazy underbelly of London's Soho in the early 1970s, where an explosion of dirty pornographic bookshops provides an open invitation for corrupt police to be greedy. With insider interviewers and archive footage, this episode continues to unearth the lawless underworld of 1970s London, audacious levels of police corruption and the birth of the internal force tasked with combating it

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: Greta sets off to the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, to call for an immediate reduction in fossil fuel use, but is frustrated by what she sees as a media obsession constantly pitting her against Donald Trump. As she travels across Europe, Greta meets Polish miners who fear that climate change policies will further decimate their industry and culture, and discovers the prototype of a technology that aims to capture carbon dioxide from factory emissions before it enters the atmosphere. She also meets one of her inspirations - Sir David Attenborough

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick discovers an unusual superstition and a rare type of Norman castle in Launceston, before meeting musicians Graham Fitkin and Ruth Wall in the far west of Cornwall

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works at a veterinary practice in rural Cumbria, learning why the health of livestock is vital to keeping the country fed, helping to keep working animals in good condition and assisting the vets who provide round-the-clock care

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: JJ Chalmers is joined by artist Chloe Kempster and blacksmith Kev Paxton as they try to transform an unwanted writing bureau, some car mechanic ramps and a hardwood coffee table

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Exploring Asian hate crime, deaf people doing jury service, still-life painting and the Deaf Mosaic exhibition. With voiceover

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Six budding gardeners and families will make regular appearances throughout the series to share hints from their own gardens all over Scotland to encourage us all to get growing. Mairi and Carole give Beechgrove a good old spring clean. While George is down at his allotment, Kirsty takes a day out at the beach to go searching for seaweed

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the penultimate episode of the quiz in which contestants choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: Christine and Jim need a garden overhaul in Urmston, Greater Manchester. In Croydon, Sara wants a new shoe cupboard, with three pairs of builders competing for the jobs

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: After what happened with Poppy and Arlo, George and Woody still are not talking, but Hannah has a radical plan to sort them out

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall visits America's Yellowstone National Park, where bears, coyotes, bison and elk roam vast grasslands, wetlands and forests. He tries to solve the puzzle of why the population of beavers increased when wolves were returned to the area after an absence of 70 years, despite the two species of animal having little to do with each another

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Monty Halls explores the extraordinary ecosystem that covers an area larger than Great Britain, revealing that only seven per cent of it is coral reef. The rest is a variety of interconnected habitats, including hundreds of islands, mangrove swamps, deep-water gardens, vast sand flats, meadows of sea grass and what is said to be the world's oldest jungle. All are interconnected by a giant deep-water lagoon - and all are full of amazing wildlife

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of the best finds from the show's travels, visiting Lulworth Castle in Dorset, Chiddingstone Castle in Kent, Wrest Park, Bedfordshire and Gloucester Cathedral. Items range from an 'Old Bill' car mascot to a large London-made clock. Plus, Paul Martin finds out more about marquetry, parquetry and inlay at Breamore House in Hampshire

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed, Dom Joly, Angela Rippon and John Robins test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's travels resume in leafy Hertfordshire, where he attempts a canoe slalom course, and visits the estate of Dame Barbara Cartland

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary delights of southern Spain, travelling from Valencia to Torremolinos and finding out about the influence of the Moors, who invaded the country in the early eighth century. Along the way, they visit one of the most fertile areas in Spain, get their hands on a freshly caught octopus, go for a mud bath and learn about flamenco, giving Dave a chance to show off his dance moves. Finally, they cook tapas for the San Juan midsummer night festival, where they reflect upon their entire Mediterranean adventure

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Dave Critchley, Dan McGeorge, Ashwani Rangta and Kirk Haworth compete for the North West of England with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. Andi Oliver welcomes them to the kitchen where they each showcase their creations, before one of them leaves the competition

programme.title: The Battle for Britney: Fans, Cash and a Conservatorship
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar attempts to untangle the complexities surrounding the controversial conservatorship Britney Spears has been under for the last 13 years. Mobeen delves into one of the most passionate fan bases in modern celebrity, attending a court hearing along the way and finding himself in the midst of the ongoing battle between Britney's conservatorship team and the passionate fans behind the #FreeBritney movement. The documentary also features never-before-seen interviews with contributors including her former choreographer Brian Friedman and celebrity blogger Perez Hilton

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Colin Lane, Sarah Millican and Noel Fielding join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'numbers' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Detectives: Fighting Organised Crime
programme.desc: When a man is gunned down on a busy residential street in Manchester, a team of specialist GMP detectives race to identify the masked men responsible

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Exploring Asian hate crime, deaf people doing jury service, still-life painting and the Deaf Mosaic exhibition. With voiceover

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Industry titans Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are entertained by a DJ and dancer pitching a socially distanced nightclub business, while an Irish inventor with a muscle massaging device hopes his proposition will loosen up the Dragons' pockets. A pair of optical consultants have their sights set on investment with their ready-readers business and a 29-year-old tech enthusiast is confident that his clever way of monetising people's personal online data. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Climate Change: Ade on the Frontline
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan visits Bangladesh's Ganges delta, which is under threat from increasingly extreme weather, and joins a government effort to plant huge new areas with mangroves to defend the low-lying country from tidal surges. He then travels to the kingdom of Bhutan to discover how melting Himalayan glaciers affect the entire world, but also hears from the prime minister how his country has become carbon negative

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets his good friend, the actor and comedian Barry Humphries, who fell in love with Cornwall in the 1960s when he escaped London to develop his famous character Dame Edna Everage. Barry talks about his friendship with British poet John Betjeman, and asks Rick to review his home-made fishcakes. Rick also goes fishing for crab, before cooking a simple yet delicious crab omelette. The host then takes a dip in the chilly north Atlantic with an eccentric group of wild water swimmers called the Perranporth Blue Tits

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Blind adventurer Amar Latif travels along one of the region's most stunning river valleys, abseiling into a hidden gorge, canoeing across a reservoir, and meeting the local llamas while following a riverside trail along the Nidd

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a valley surrounded by mountains at the start of spring, when the snow begins to melt and flow across the landscape

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate the origins of two paintings by unknown artists dating back to the 18th and 19th centuries, which unusually for the period, depict black subjects. One is a portrait of Dido Belle, a famous former slave adopted into an aristocratic English family in the 1760s, while the other depicts two children against a tropical landscape, and is believed to have been painted as a protest against the slave trade

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: Mark looks at some of the best films about British history in the order of when they are set, tracing the story of Britain from the Roman invasion to the modern era, via medieval times, the Tudors, the Civil War and the 18th and 19th centuries

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the evolution of the weather forecast, from print via radio to TV and beyond, featuring an insight into how the Met Office and the BBC have always used the latest technology to bring the holy grail of accurate forecasting that much closer. Yet, as hand-drawn maps have been replaced by weather apps, the bigger drama of global warming has been playing itself out, as if to prove that people were right all along to obsess about the weather

programme.title: Rich Hall's Working for the American Dream
programme.desc: Rich Hall explores the ethos of the American Dream, and uncovers how it has been explored and perpetuated by politicians, industrialists, writers and artists

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a valley surrounded by mountains at the start of spring, when the snow begins to melt and flow across the landscape

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Blind adventurer Amar Latif travels along one of the region's most stunning river valleys, abseiling into a hidden gorge, canoeing across a reservoir, and meeting the local llamas while following a riverside trail along the Nidd

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?

One is a portrait of Dido Belle, a famous former slave adopted into an aristocratic English family in the 1760s, while the other depicts two children against a tropical landscape, and is believed to have been painted as a protest against the slave trade

programme.title: Timeshift

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Former Coronation Street star Shobna Gulati takes a stroll through Shropshire and Worcestershire along the banks of the Severn, Great Britain's longest river, finding out about a part of the country that is a world away from her native north-west England

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scenic river landscape, capturing the natural world surrounding it on canvas

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Hacker sees a chance to further his political career when a new hospital appears to have no patients. Satirical comedy, starring Paul Eddington, Nigel Hawthorne and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy. The death of her husband leaves upper-class snob Audrey fforbes-Hamilton a widow in drastically reduced circumstances, with her beloved ancestral pile up for auction. Starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: The Violence Paradox
programme.desc: Psychologist Steven Pinker journeys through history and the human mind to explore what triggers violence and how it may have decreased over time. In the first of two programmes, research includes clues from a prehistoric archaeological dig in Kenya, an experiment to test the moral judgement of babies and data analysis of 239 years of Old Bailey proceedings

programme.title: The Violence Paradox
programme.desc: In the second of the two-part documentary, psychologist Steven Pinker examines evidence that suggests the world is becoming more peaceful, including how income equality and personal contact through sport may curb violence in modern societies

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Research suggests the connections in men's and women's brains follow different patterns, which might explain typical forms of gender-specific behaviour. Dr Michael Mosley and Professor Alice Roberts investigate whether these are innate or are shaped by the outside world, using a team of human guinea pigs and a troop of Barbary apes to test the science and challenge old stereotypes. They ask whether this new scientific research will benefit both sexes or drive them even further apart

programme.title: Tales of Winter: The Art of Snow and Ice
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how man's struggle with the onset of winter has been depicted by Western painters across the centuries. Among the works featured is Pieter Bruegel the Elder's Hunters in the Snow from 1565 - one of the first paintings to sum up the season as a vision of beauty. With contributions by Grayson Perry, Will Self and Don McCullin, among others

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scenic river landscape, capturing the natural world surrounding it on canvas

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Former Coronation Street star Shobna Gulati takes a stroll through Shropshire and Worcestershire along the banks of the Severn, Great Britain's longest river, finding out about a part of the country that is a world away from her native north-west England

programme.title: The Violence Paradox
programme.desc: Psychologist Steven Pinker journeys through history and the human mind to explore what triggers violence and how it may have decreased over time. In the first of two programmes, research includes clues from a prehistoric archaeological dig in Kenya, an experiment to test the moral judgement of babies and data analysis of 239 years of Old Bailey proceedings

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Jemma Woodman goes on a culinary tour of the river Dart, meeting those who get inspiration and food from this scenic part of the South West.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross has more painting tips as he captures on canvas a picturesque spot in the meadow, with a couple of serene fishing holes

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts shares the best archaeological discoveries of 2018, starting with the north of the UK and a preserved prehistoric village at the black Loch in Myrton. The programme also follows archaeologists uncovering a previously unknown Anglo-Saxon cemetery in Lincolnshire and a major regeneration project in Salford, unearthing the largest jail in Georgian England

programme.title: The 1951 Festival of Britain: Brave New World
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how the 1951 Festival of Britain helped the nation to look forward to a brighter future during a period of post-war austerity. Archive footage and personal testimonies reveal how, despite widespread debt and rationing, the event encouraged people to have fun through design and ingenuity

programme.title: The Treasure Hunters
programme.desc: Dallas Campbell and Ellie Harrison present the first of two programmes in which they embark on the ultimate treasure hunt, searching the globe to uncover its extraordinary riches, from natural gems to precious metals - and more besides. Dallas begins his search Down Under, free-diving for lustrous pearls in the waters around north-west Australia and seeking out diamonds from the bottom of the ocean. He also learns about opal mining in one of the most hostile places on Earth and, together with Ellie, reveals how ambergris, a substance that starts life in a sperm whale's stomach, can prove an expensive ingredient in perfume. Plus, how one of the largest and most unusual treasures has helped solve a 67-million-year-old puzzle

programme.title: Handmade
programme.desc: Filmed over five days, this programme examines the complex and time-consuming process involved in building a classic Windsor chair, made by craftsman Jim Steele in his Warwickshire workshop. The traditional design features woods chosen for their different qualities - ash, elm and yew - and the finished object has just two screws and no glue

programme.title: Bacchus Uncovered: Ancient God of Ecstasy
programme.desc: Professor Bettany Hughes investigates the story of Bacchus, travelling to Georgia, Britain, Jordan and Greece to discover his origins and his presence in the modern world

programme.title: Mystic
programme.desc: Issie gets a new vision of Mystic running through the forest along an old railway track, and wonders if this might be the clue that will lead her to Blaze’s herd. After some encouragement, Issie decides to ride out on Blaze to find the tracks, and Caroline, Caleb, Dan and Natasha join her on the mission. As they journey deeper into the forest, past their favourite swimming hole, the kids make a series of bewildering discoveries and eventually realise they’re completely and utterly lost. With one of the gang’s future ambitions at stake, they face a race against time to get back to the stables. Despite their best efforts to hide what happened, the consequences of their mission have serious consequences for them all as Tom presents the group with a difficult dilemma. programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: My Dog Ears follows the journey of 12-year-old Joe who is hoping to receive a very precious gift - a dog that will become his ears. Joe has been profoundly deaf since he was a baby and a specially trained hearing dog could bring him the independence and confidence he so craves. Only 30 children in the UK have one of these dogs and Joe is next on the waiting list, but his journey isn't straightforward. Joe also has a Titanium leg and needs an operation to help him walk before he can receive his dog. Can Joe fully recover from his surgery and complete the training that will allow him to have a new best friend - his very own hearing dog?

programme.desc: It’s Halloween and Mimi is on a mission to prank her best friend, Ashleigh, who claims she is not afraid of anything. She has found a spooky cave rumoured to be haunted by a ghost searching for his own heart, which was stolen several hundred years ago. Mimi also introduces sisters Angel, Melody and Nevaeh, who are super-proud of their traveller heritage and go trick or treating on their plot of land. Another Halloween fan is TikTok phenomenon Fats Timbo, who is Mimi’s Q&A guest and tells us how she has managed to use her platform to empower herself and others. Ashleigh is unimpressed when she finally arrives at the cave - until a surprise visitor gives her the fright of her life.

programme.title: Odd Squad

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: My Dog Ears follows the journey of 12-year-old Joe who is hoping to receive a very precious gift - a dog that will become his ears. Joe has been profoundly deaf since he was a baby and a specially trained hearing dog could bring him the independence and confidence he so craves. Only 30 children in the UK have one of these dogs and Joe is next on the waiting list, but his journey isn't straightforward. Joe also has a Titanium leg and needs an operation to help him walk before he can receive his dog. Can Joe fully recover from his surgery and complete the training that will allow him to have a new best friend - his very own hearing dog?

Before the big moment, Charlie wraps up a cuddly elephant toy with his Grandma as a gift for his new brother or sister. programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Stevie and Conor as they begin to discover the world of sound and see how their hearing is developed. Conor’s big brother Harrison is here to help show him around all his different musical toys, including a noisy rattle. In the garden, Stevie hangs out with mum and dad and listens to all the different sounds before getting ready to go out. Meanwhile, Conor heads over to grandma’s house while listening for sounds with his mum and brother. Stevie gets a quick hearing test at the hospital before heading home to make lots of noise with household objects. After making rainmakers at grandma’s house, Harrison and Conor get hold of a set of walkie talkies to play with. When the day is almost finished, it’s time for Stevie's parents to sing her a lullaby to help send her to sleep.

Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Nigel has a book inside his bag. All the parents and babies have books with different words and pictures too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to hide a baby-appropriate book inside a fabric bag or pillowcase to explore with their baby. Some viewers at home show how they’ve been reading books and sharing stories too. The nursery rhyme This Is the Way We Read a Book features, as does a story about Baby Bear’s favourite book. The Together Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Derek Brockway visits south Wales and discovers a walk of contrasts that includes a beach, a canal and a bustling marina. On the way he learns about Welsh tarantulas and narrowly avoids crashing a luxury boat, while wildlife expert Lizzie Daly goes on a bat hunt and party planner Onkar Singh Purewal organises a tea-party in a ghost village

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher works on a mantle clock in the shape of a ship, a treasured gift from the owner's late grandfather. Kirsten Ramsay is called upon to painstakingly piece together a vintage ceramic night light, fractured into dozens of fragments, and skateboard enthusiast Mike is hoping Will Kirk and Dominic Chinea can restore his wooden board, which transports him back to his youth in 1960s Liverpool. Finally, toy restorers Julie and Amanda undertake the repair of a treasured teddy bear that has been by the side of a young man as he's undergone life-changing surgery

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contestants go global, taking on garments and techniques from around the world. For the pattern challenge they make a French breton top - and judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young will be looking for perfectly matched stripes and a completely flat neck - before they transform sarongs into completely different garments. In the final round, the sewers must come up with an outfit inspired by Mexican artist Frida Kahlo, known as much for her personal style as for her vivid artwork. Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Reporter Greg McKenzie meets young people born or brought up in the UK who say Home Office immigration policy treats them as second class citizens. They cannot vote, cannot claim benefits, struggle to go to university and may even face deportation. Panorama investigates Home Office policy which gives them a chance to stay in the UK, but which takes 10 years and costs thousands of pounds

programme.title: Unsolved: An Alibi for Omar?
programme.desc: Journalist Bronagh Munro uncovers new evidence casting doubt on the conviction of a man who has spent 19 years in prison for murder. Omar Benguit was jailed for killing 26-year-old Korean student Jong-Ok Shin in Bournemouth in 2002. Now, Bronagh tracks down some of the alleged witnesses to ask them what they remember. She also finds evidence that could give Omar Benguit an alibi for the night of the murder

programme.desc: Dion Dublin looks round a chalet-style bungalow in the Derbyshire village of Kilburn and a three-bedroom end terrace in Huthwaite, Nottinghamshire that offers a colourful reminder of his footballing past. Plus, a revisit to a church in Dover that Martin Roberts first saw five years ago

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works at a veterinary practice in rural Cumbria, learning why the health of livestock is vital to keeping the country fed, helping to keep working animals in good condition and assisting the vets who provide round-the-clock care

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Two police officers and an air stewardess perform CPR on a lorry driver who has had a cardiac arrest at the wheel. A mother calls mountain rescue when her son falls from a tree

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning visits the Elsecar Heritage Centre in South Yorkshire, where Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley help two teams scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction. Anita also learns about a local man who changed the face of mining in Britain

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi helps a family struggling with the long-term physical and emotional effects of cancer over the course of a decade. Adrian Lewis Morgan stars

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple of Londoners who have £1million to spend on a detached property in the Essex countryside with lots of space for family and friends. Away from the search, Ginny visits a farmer who has found that the county's unique climate has enabled him to grow a crop usually found much further afield

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: JJ Chalmers is joined by artist Chloe Kempster and blacksmith Kev Paxton as they try to transform an unwanted writing bureau, car mechanic ramps and a hardwood coffee table

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a pair of binoculars salvaged from a Second World War U-Boat, two leather boxing belts and a vintage mechanical money box

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A furious Ben demands answers from Callum after his confession. Linda speaks to Nancy's doctor, but her meddling only makes things worse between mother and daughter. Keegan makes an error that could jeopardise the search for Bailey and Kim assesses Bobby's Fox Catcher application. Despite only being here five minutes, Stuart's grandma Vi seems to be getting on everyone's nerves - although she insists that Walford is growing on her

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The cause of Reyhan's death prompts an investigation, putting Hanssen and Sahira's already brittle relationship at breaking point. The success of Dominic's operation gives him a new-found sense of hope for the future, while Kian continues to lie to Lucky about notifying social services regarding Andrei

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate the case of a man who thought he had found love on a dating site with a woman who began making demands for money

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The gang arrive back in England, devastated that their trip to Monaco has been all for nothing. However, as they leave the airport, they find an unexpected welcome party waiting for them with big news. Keeley realises that Jake may still has feelings for Georgina, and as Frank's lies begin to unravel, he begs Cheryl to help him in a last-ditch attempt to save himself and Duke

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The hopefuls are given a challenge by Cate Hall, the lead hair and make-up designer on award-winning drama The Crown, demonstrating their knowledge of period make-up to create authentic and recognisable looks for a screen test. In the creative brief, they are challenged to turn their models into a living piece of art

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: A celebration of the entrepreneurial spirit among the young. This opening episode features Nanial Jones, who invested all his savings into a vintage clothing business. Plus, Alaw Haf who now makes £5,000 a month from subscriptions on the OnlyFans website, having lost her gym job in lockdown, and Toni Leigh Hall who has launched a new line of humorous cards, calendars, door-mats, mouse-mats and face masks

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: The prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho returns, with Emma and Norman each making a decision about the future, and Norma setting boundaries for her son. An unexpected visitor puts Dylan's fresh start in jeopardy. Starring Freddie Highmore and Vera Farmiga

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: The mother and son fixate on Annika's disappearance, and Norma uncovers the truth about the Arcanum Club. Dylan and Caleb meet their new neighbour. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga, Freddie Highmore and Max Thieriot

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford hears how some missing parcels can end up being sold off at auction instead of safely delivered. Plus, the rural communities who struggled to get quality broadband

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts explores an ambitious housing development in Tredegar, Dion Dublin visits a house in Great Barr and Martel Maxwell is impressed by a six-bedroom terrace in Liverpool

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works with one of the UK's biggest water companies. He traces the flow of this precious resource from the reservoir to homes and businesses, before finding out what happens with wastewater at the other end of the system

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A father braves a house fire to save his daughter. A pregnant woman on the way to hospital does not make it, so her partner dials 999 when the baby begins to arrive in the car

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents as two teams search for antiques in Leominster, Herefordshire, led by experts Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks. Christina learns about 'The Leominster Chelsea Bun', which travelled to the battle fields of the First World War and back

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie faces a choice - does she want to a medical career or a life of showbusiness glitz and glamour? A young carer struggles to cope when his grandma's obsession with an old music hall performance gets out of hand

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson searches for a Cambridgeshire home for a young couple with a £570,000 budget who want to escape suburban Kent. Keen to explore more of the country's wildlife, Jules also visits the Fens and finds out about the UK's first nature reserves

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore enlists screen print designer Daniel Heath and clock restorer Paul Parry to rejuvenate an old writing bureau and a 1940s mantle clock. She also tries to cultivate something sellable from fragrant bags of garden waste

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Vintage bike restorer Tim Gunn brings all his skill to bear on a wartime bicycle once owned and used by a Bletchley Park code breaker. Horologist Steve Fletcher recruits fellow expert and sister Suzie to work on a neglected and battered old Victorian firefighter's helmet. A threadbare teddy on wheels is also restored

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Home Ground
programme.desc: Gavin Andrews travels to a hemp farm in Markethill, Co Armagh, to find out about the sustainable crop, while Jo Scott is in Co Fermanagh to meet a butcher who keeps his herd of pigs on an island. Ruth Sanderson joins the team at Movanagher Fish Farm to learn how Northern Ireland angling estates are stocked up. and wildlife expert Conor McKinney learns about cannon netting, a specialised technique for monitoring bird numbers

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher works on a mantle clock in the shape of a ship, a treasured gift from the owner's late grandfather. Kirsten Ramsay is called upon to painstakingly piece together a vintage ceramic night light, fractured into dozens of fragments, and skateboard enthusiast Mike is hoping Will Kirk and Dominic Chinea can restore his wooden board, which transports him back to his youth in 1960s Liverpool. Finally, toy restorers Julie and Amanda undertake the repair of a treasured teddy bear that has been by the side of a young man as he's undergone life-changing surgery

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contestants go global, taking on garments and techniques from around the world. For the pattern challenge they make a French breton top - and judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young will be looking for perfectly matched stripes and a completely flat neck - before they transform sarongs into completely different garments. In the final round, the sewers must come up with an outfit inspired by Mexican artist Frida Kahlo, known as much for her personal style as for her vivid artwork. Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Nolan Live
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates, with viewers sending in their questions via text, email and tweet

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Reporter Greg McKenzie meets young people born or brought up in the UK who say Home Office immigration policy treats them as second class citizens. They cannot vote, cannot claim benefits, struggle to go to university and may even face deportation. Panorama investigates Home Office policy which gives them a chance to stay in the UK, but which takes 10 years and costs thousands of pounds

programme.title: Unsolved: An Alibi for Omar?
programme.desc: Journalist Bronagh Munro uncovers new evidence casting doubt on the conviction of a man who has spent 19 years in prison for murder. Omar Benguit was jailed for killing 26-year-old Korean student Jong-Ok Shin in Bournemouth in 2002. Now, Bronagh tracks down some of the alleged witnesses to ask them what they remember. She also finds evidence that could give Omar Benguit an alibi for the night of the murder

programme.title: Boxing: Canelo Alvarez
programme.desc: A profile of the Mexican boxer, who has won world titles in four different weight classes

programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: Talking Movies reports on the excitement at Union Station in Los Angeles, which is co-hosting this year's Academy Awards. Several big name stars tell the programme team how they view the 2021 Oscars contest, which has the most diverse field of contenders ever. British actor Riz Ahmed discusses the significance of becoming the first Muslim to be nominated for a best actor Oscar. Also, celebrated Danish film-maker Thomas Vinterberg discusses his drinking drama Another Round, favoured to win for best international feature film. The programme will also reveal what millions of viewers around the world can expect to see when they tune in to watch this in-person Oscars ceremony, which has been significantly reinvented because of the global pandemic.

programme.title: Dan
programme.desc: Catriona Murray talks about how poetry can evoke memories of a person. She reads “An Luchag ‘s an Cat” which immediately reminds her of her cousin who is no longer alive

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Sgeul Seolaidh
programme.desc: The story of the significant role played by Gaels in the British Merchant Navy. Sailors from the Highlands and Islands reminisce, providing a unique insight into what it was like to sail the deep seas

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)
programme.desc: Heather Dewar travels to Gairloch, where she meets local radio presenter and former teacher Alice Mackenzie, and draws a colour portrait of her

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Hamish Taylor reveals how the Monach Isles - a group of fertile machair islands, five miles to the west of North Uist - have a special bond with Grimsay and its people

programme.title: Na Trads
programme.desc: Robert Robertson presents music from the Trad Awards and from past Trad Winners, with Sheila Stewart, Griogair Labhruidh, Lori Watson and Karine Polwart among others

programme.title: Sar-sgeoil: Outlander
programme.desc: Cathy MacDonald explores the locations, folklore and history that inspired Diana Gabaldon's hugely successful Outlander, outlining the facts behind the fiction that has brought fans flocking to Scotland from across the globe

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: The lads contemplate winter and come up with heart-warming comfort foods. Ruairidh cooks red cabbage and a lamb hotpot with mint sauce, and Uisdean prepares a mutton chump roast

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Gudrun - A' Bhana-phrionnsa Lochlannach
programme.desc: The princess races through the woods and comes face to face with a wolf

programme.title: Peataichean/Pets
programme.desc: Eilidh is joined by Alec and Doris, his pet Burmese cat

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: The Tuna Fish tells us how speedy they are and how they are good hunters. They hunt with their friends, the dolphins, as the dolphins keep them safe from other predators

programme.title: Sionnach agus Maigheach (Fox & Hare)
programme.desc: Fox wants to give Hare the best birthday ever. Owl and Tusk are invited, but only if they bring a nice present, like a beautifully scented flower

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Shanes want to find hazelnuts for his dish of the day so he, Izzy and Mario head to the woods to forage for some

programme.title: Treud na Dlùth-choille - Grad-Naidheachd (Jungle Bunch)
programme.desc: Maurice wants Òigear to show his ferocious side when he takes him on a hunting lesson. Animation for younger viewers

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: Seren is chosen to go in search of cherries

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Morag Ann MacNeil reads Air a' Chladach by Anna Milbourne, illustrated by Erica-Jane Waters and translated into Gaelic by Donald John MacLeod

programme.title: Ronia, Nighean a' Mheirlich/Ronja, the Robber's Daughter
programme.desc: Ronia and Biorc think that there is nowhere as delightful as the forest. They would like to tame some horses so that they can go riding – and this will require dedication

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Olaidh and Coinneach are in Russia but skiing isn’t the only thing that’s going on - there’s a tank to be seen, soldiers marching and a game of skittles

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Anna reveals her very unusual hobby

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: Danger Mouse, Penfold, Squawk and Colonel K must team up with accidental duplicates of themselves to stop Big Head

programme.title: Fuine (Home Baking)
programme.desc: Mairi MacRitchie enlists the help of three skilled cooks to prepare a festive feast, including mince pies, a marzipan-filled stollen and a rich Christmas cake for pudding. Plus, recipes for cocktails and canapes will be shared, and ideas for edible gifts and decorations

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.desc: Calum Maclean walks up the gorge at Corrieshalloch hoping that something catches his eye for that special photo

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: When vandalism strikes the Bay the day before the town is judged on its beauty, it is up to the Provost to clean up

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Seona Brown sings Ghràidh ar Dè, O Ghràidh gun Choimeas, and John Archie MacMillan quotes from the renowned speech by Martin Luther King, I Have a Dream

programme.desc: Rita and Crocodile are playing croquet in the garden, but Crocodile is much better at playing than Rita

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing has been very busy creating a beautiful chalk drawing of a dinosaur outside

programme.title: Tree Fu Tom

programme.title: Su Shiusaidh (Little Suzy's Zoo)
programme.desc: The friends discover a new game - following one another around

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Iain MacInnes reads Caiptean Wag agus na Mathain Bhàna written and illustrated by Michael Terry and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda
programme.desc: Tìgreas comes down with a dangerous disease and there is only one cure to be had - a rare flower that happens to be in the hands of an evil scorpion. Po and Muncaidh set out get the flower from her clutches in time to save Tìgreas' life

programme.title: Gaemannan Gaidhealach
programme.desc: Coinneach Cllogaid tries to take advantage of the others

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to make a portable bug-catcher

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Scott Rigg operates on a cat, Fiona Crowden tries to save a calf's life near Blairgowrie, and Kay and Hector are busy with cats in Stornoway

programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Dan
programme.desc: Iain MacKenzie talks about the link between poetry and place as he reads Derick Thomson’s poem “Air Mòinteach Shuardail”

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: An insight, through detailed personal accounts, of Christmas in 2020, a time which is usually important for families coming together, but was greatly impacted by the Coronavirus

programme.title: Sar Sgeoil
programme.desc: Cathy MacDonald visits Culduie in Applecross - the small crofting township in which Graeme Macrae Burnet's critically acclaimed novel His Bloody Project is set. There, she investigates the fact behind the fiction in this dark tale

programme.title: Mach à Seo!
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon heads to Argyll's Slate Islands. Known as the 'Islands that Roofed the World', he learns about the rich history of the slate quarrying industry

programme.title: Sgeulachd Deacon Brodie
programme.desc: John Morrison explores the life of Edinburgh-born William Brodie, whose criminal dealings and double identity is believed to have inspired Robert Louis Stevenson's character Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde. The presenter investigates why the ambiguous man who was hanged for his affairs in 1889 is still regarded with affection in the Scottish capital, with the help of expert opinion and drama reconstruction

programme.title: Na Trads
programme.desc: Robert Robertson presents music from the Trad Awards and from past Trad Winners, with Sheila Stewart, Griogair Labhruidh, Lori Watson and Karine Polwart among others

programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: The Secret Life of Pets (2016)
programme.desc: When rivalry leaves them cast adrift on the streets of New York, two bickering hounds must put their differences aside and pull together. Animated adventure from the makers of Despicable Me, featuring the voices of Louis CK, Eric Stonestreet and Kevin Hart

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Sally Lindsay hosts as five workmates from Devon attempt to answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, before trying to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Karen, Joe, Nav and Sarah pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Conservative Party

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Moira and Cain set a plan in motion. Charity gives Mackenzie a warning. Mackenzie and Aaron are in trouble

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Harvey's heavies watch Carla and Peter meet in a car park, and Abi's day goes from bad to worse with a series of setbacks sending her stress levels through the roof

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Former ballerina Darcey Bussell joins Alan to talk about bringing dance back to the nation this summer, while carpenter Wayne Perrey creates a garden bar. Plus, a couple of showstopping rare breed Gloucester cows make their TV debuts

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sharon calls Harvey's heavy with details of Leanne's hideout, and Nina mentions to Asha that she saw Corey openly flirting with Kelly in Speed Daal

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Des O'Connor: The Ultimate Entertainer
programme.desc: A tribute to the longest-serving prime time TV host of all time, who topped theatre bills globally, including more than a thousand solo appearances at the London Palladium. And despite much mockery from Morecambe and Wise, he released 36 chart-topping albums. The programme tells how Des went from Butlins Redcoat to become the ultimate entertainer, and features contributions from celebrity friends and admirers including Jonathan Ross, Jay Leno, Melanie Sykes, Bradley Walsh, Joe Pasquale, Alan Davies, Elaine Paige and Cliff Richard
